message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German Election Study – GLES 2021 (Post-election cross-sectional survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13891","justification":"Covers political behaviour and attitudes in Germany around the 2021 federal election, including conflictual issues, trust, security-related attitudes, and evaluations of international politics and organizations."},{"title":"German Election Study – GLES 2021 (Campaign panel survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13890","justification":"Panel data on voters in Germany during the 2021 election campaign, with rich measures of political behaviour, conflictual issue positions, party evaluations, and attitudes toward international politics."},{"title":"German Election Study – GLES Rolling Cross-Section 2021","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13938","justification":"Rolling cross-section survey of eligible voters in Germany in 2021 with detailed information on political attitudes and behaviour, including issue conflict and views on security and international affairs."},{"title":"German Federal Election 2021 – Politbarometer (ZA7780)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13929","justification":"Regular Politbarometer surveys around the 2021 federal election in Germany, measuring political behaviour, voting intentions, and attitudes towards parties, policies, and international politics."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2020 (ZA7691)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=7691&db=e&doi=10.4232/1.13838","justification":"German Politbarometer series wave for 2020 with variables on political preferences, conflictual domestic issues, and views on international politics and organizations."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2022 (ZA7883)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14079","justification":"Covers political attitudes and behaviour of the German population in 2022, including responses to international security issues and political conflict in Germany and abroad."},{"title":"Politbarometer 2023 (ZA8015)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14225","justification":"Measures political behaviour and attitudes in Germany in 2023, including reactions to international conflicts, security concerns, and evaluations of international organizations."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021 – Short-term Campaign Panel (ZA7708)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=7708&db=e&doi=10.4232/1.13889","justification":"Short-term panel around the 2021 election in Germany with detailed attitudinal measures on parties, issues, and international politics during a period of heightened political conflict."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study – Online Panel 2021–2022 (GLES Panel 2021–2022)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14112","justification":"Follows respondents in Germany from the 2021 election into 2022, capturing dynamics of political attitudes, conflict perceptions, and positions on international and security-related topics."},{"title":"Germany, COVID-19 Snapshot Monitoring (COSMO) – Political Attitudes and Trust (selected waves 2020–2022)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13984","justification":"German multi-wave survey on the COVID-19 pandemic including trust in political institutions, risk perceptions, and attitudes toward government measures and international organizations in the early 2020s."}]}
